pages.title: Primary Classes
pages.text: Eight primary classes. You pick exactly one at world creation. Every item the class owns has had its normal recipe removed; the class recipe is the only legal way to craft it.

pages.title: Combat (5)
pages.text: Flat catalogues. No subclasses. Self-contained.$(br)$(li)$(l:classes/mage)Mage$(/l) — three schools, one caster.$(li)$(l:classes/monk)Monk$(/l) — holy plate, holy staves, magnum torches.$(li)$(l:classes/warrior)Warrior$(/l) — the leanest catalogue, signature plate only.$(li)$(l:classes/ranger)Ranger$(/l) — bows, crossbows, spears, scout armor.$(li)$(l:classes/rogue)Rogue$(/l) — shadow armor, daggers, grave-sentinel set.

pages.title: Craft (3)
pages.text: $(li)$(l:classes/engineer)Engineer$(/l) — pick a discipline: $(l:classes/eng_aeronautical)Aero$(/l), $(l:classes/eng_mechanical)Mech$(/l), $(l:classes/eng_electrical)Elec$(/l).$(li)$(l:classes/armorer)Armorer$(/l) — generic plate, shields, themed sets.$(li)$(l:classes/blacksmith)Blacksmith$(/l) — generic melee weapons, claymores, daggers, polearms.

pages.title: Recipes are harder
pages.text: Class recipes are strictly harder than the mod's original — extra steps, higher tier, or rarer materials. JEI shows the exact grid.$(br2)Each class has a matching trader who sells the same catalogue for $(l:traders/runes)runes$(/l). Other classes use the trader as their cross-class shortcut.
